𝗙𝗥𝗘𝗘-𝗖𝗢𝗗𝗜𝗡𝗚-𝗠𝗢𝗗𝗘𝗟𝗦 | To quickly identify and select the most performant free Large Language Models (LLMs) specifically for coding tasks, enabling developers to choose the best model for their needs based on real-time latency benchmarks.  🔥 Super Hot!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📜 JavaScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To quickly identify and select the most performant free Large Language Models (LLMs) specifically for coding tasks, enabling developers to choose the best model for their needs based on real-time latency benchmarks.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Find, benchmark, and install free coding LLM models
✅ Supports 159+ models across 20 providers (NVIDIA NIM, Groq, Hugging Face, etc.)
✅ Real-time parallel pinging for latency testing
✅ Smart ranking to highlight top 3 fastest models
✅ Settings screen for API key management, provider enablement, proxy configuration
✅ Multi-account proxy (`fcm-proxy`) for grouping accounts and auto-rotation
✅ Coding-focused models only


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 582 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 54 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 13 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

This JavaScript CLI tool helps developers find, benchmark, and install free coding-focused LLM models from over 20 providers. It features real-time parallel pinging to test model latency and a smart ranking system to highlight the fastest options, with additional features for proxy management and multi-account support.

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/vava-nessa/free-coding-models

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗚𝗜𝗧-𝗠𝗨𝗫 | To provide a comprehensive GitHub repository browsing and management experience within the terminal, enhancing developer productivity by reducing context switching.  

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🦀 Rust 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a comprehensive GitHub repository browsing and management experience within the terminal, enhancing developer productivity by reducing context switching.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Terminal UI dashboard for GitHub
✅ Browse repositories
✅ View commit history, PRs, issues, CI status
✅ Profile overview with contribution heatmap
✅ Repository cards with key metrics
✅ Stats panel with commit streak and languages
✅ Filter and search repositories
✅ Repo detail view with comprehensive metrics
✅ Start work: clone, branch, and open in tmux or locally
✅ Notification indicator
✅ Startup cache for fast reloads
✅ Configurable exclusions


❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

⭐⭐ 30 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 0 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Git-mux is a terminal UI dashboard built with Rust for browsing GitHub repositories. It allows users to view repository details, commit history, pull requests, issues, CI status, and contribution statistics directly within their terminal.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/nooesc/git-mux

𝗧𝗥𝗘𝗡𝗗𝗦-𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞𝗘𝗥 | To analyze and visualize Google Trends data for market research, demand validation, trend tracking, and opportunity discovery through an easy-to-use CLI tool.  🌟 Rising Fast!

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To analyze and visualize Google Trends data for market research, demand validation, trend tracking, and opportunity discovery through an easy-to-use CLI tool.
📅 Created: Aug 2025 • Updated: Mar 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Multi-Region Analysis
✅ Flexible Timeframes
✅ Multiple Search Categories (Web, YouTube, Images, News, Shopping)
✅ Custom Keyword Input (direct or file-based)
✅ Display Options (vertical, wide table)
✅ Data Export to CSV
✅ Handling of Google Trends rate limiting (429 errors) via cookie authentication
✅ Analysis of related rising queries


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 127 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 13 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Trends Checker is a Python-based command-line interface (CLI) tool designed for analyzing Google Trends data. It offers advanced features like rate limiting, cookie authentication, and visually appealing terminal output, built upon the pytrends library to provide enterprise-grade trend analysis capabilities.

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗦𝗖𝗥𝗔𝗣𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗚 | Automated data extraction from websites through flexible and scalable web scraping and crawling.  🔥 Super Hot!

•••••••••••••••••••••••

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Automated data extraction from websites through flexible and scalable web scraping and crawling.
📅 Created: Oct 2024 • Updated: Mar 2026

•••••••••••••••••••••••

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Adaptive Web Scraping
✅ Full-scale Crawling
✅ Handles single requests to large crawls
✅ Playwright integration
✅ Stealth capabilities
✅ Support for various selectors (XPath, CSS)
✅ Proxy rotation
✅ Command-Line Interface (CLI)
✅ AI Agent Skill integration


•••••••••••••••••••••••

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 29.7K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2.3K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 3 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

•••••••••••••••••••••••

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Scrapling is a Python framework designed for adaptive web scraping and crawling. It offers a flexible and robust solution for extracting data from websites, handling tasks ranging from single page requests to large-scale crawls.

𝗚𝗘𝗢𝗔𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗧 | Accurately determining the geographic location of an image using visual and textual reasoning.  🔥 Super Hot!

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Accurately determining the geographic location of an image using visual and textual reasoning.
📅 Created: Feb 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Image geolocation at multiple geographic granularities
✅ Reasoning with interpretable chain-of-thought
✅ Geo-similarity reward for spatial and semantic matching
✅ Consistency reward for reasoning integrity
✅ GeoSeek dataset for training and validation


❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 63 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 6 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

GeoAgent is a vision-language model designed for image geolocation, capable of determining locations at various granularities (city, region, country, continent). It leverages a Qwen2.5-VL base and incorporates novel reward mechanisms for improved reasoning and accuracy, trained on a specialized dataset called GeoSeek.

❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂❂

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/HVision-NKU/GeoAgent
